page: Amazon Web Services c6g.large is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom 64-bit ARM architecture. It features 2 dedicated physical cores on the AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz, with 1 thread per core. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates moderate single-core capabilities alongside notable multi-core and memory constraints. The stress-ng single-core CPU test shows average performance at 1519.49, but multi-core performance drops to a weak rating of 3010.97. Memory bandwidth is mixed; while large block read-only bandwidth is average at 38.93 GB/sec, small block cached reads and large block writes are rated poor and weak respectively. Cryptographic capabilities are supported via hardware AES, but the processor lacks AVX or AMX vector extensions, resulting in poor scores in Passmark CPU tests and Geekbench multi-core workloads.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64-based Graviton architecture offers a lower-cost entry point for dedicated compute resources compared to traditional x86 instances. However, tradeoffs include the lack of local storage, limited 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and lower performance in memory-intensive or highly parallelized tasks. This instance is best suited for lightweight compute workloads, basic web serving, single-threaded applications, and development or testing environments optimized for ARM architectures.
description: Amazon Web Services c6g.large is a compute-optimized instance featuring 2 dedicated physical cores on an ARM64 AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 processor running at 2.5 GHz. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 4.0 GB of system memory, 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, and no local storage or GPUs. Benchmark results show average single-core CPU performance with a stress-ng score of 1519.49, but multi-core and memory write speeds are relatively weak. Lacking AVX and AMX extensions, it exhibits lower efficiency in vector and cryptographic operations. This instance offers a cost-effective entry point for dedicated ARM64 compute resources, making it suitable for lightweight web serving, single-threaded processing, and ARM-targeted development environments.

tagline: A dedicated ARM64 compute instance featuring custom Graviton processors for lightweight, cost-efficient single-threaded workloads and development environments.
bullet_points: 2 dedicated ARM64 physical cores on AWS Graviton Neoverse-N1 at 2.5 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core
1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and Nitro hypervisor integration
Average single-core CPU performance with weak multi-core scaling
No local storage or integrated GPU hardware included
Best suited for lightweight web serving and ARM development tasks
